title: pseudoxandra obscurinervis
intro: Pseudoxandra obscurinervis is a species of plant in the Annonaceae family, commonly known as the custard apple family, native to the tropical forests of the Congo Basin. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Pseudoxandra obscurinervis is typically used in the form of a decoction or infusion, where the dried leaves and stems are steeped in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
